textRun.content: If you want business to be plug-and-play, you have to stop thinking in terms of companies and start thinking in terms of modules, interfaces, and constraints.
What you’re really asking for is a Business Operating Template (BOT) — a repeatable system where ideas, people, and capital can be swapped without breaking the machine.
Below is the cleanest way to do this at a systems-engineering level, not hustle fluff.
Every business must be decomposed into replaceable modules with defined inputs, outputs, and governance rules.
If a component cannot be replaced without collapsing the business, it is not a system — it’s a personality-dependent operation.
Every plug-and-play business runs on the same 6 core layers:
Think of this like a motherboard. Every venture snaps into the same slots.
Purpose: Capture attention → qualify demand → convert.
Never custom-build this per business. Ever.
Rule: The interface stays the same; only the message swaps.
Purpose: Transform input (customer state) into output (result).
Every offer must be expressible as:
If it needs explanation longer than 60 seconds, it’s not a module.
This is where most founders break the system.
The founder never delivers by default. They only design delivery.
If delivery quality depends on “talent,” it’s not templatized yet.
Growth must be independent from delivery.
You are not scaling a business — you’re scaling loops.
If a human leaves and knowledge leaves with them, the system failed.
This prevents chaos as you add businesses.
Shared services layer (marketing, ops, AI, finance)
Venture → Holding Co → Allocation Engine → Reinvestment
A business is plug-and-play only if:
✅ You can swap the traffic source
✅ You can pause growth without collapse
✅ You can sell the unit without explaining it
If any answer is “no,” it’s still artisanal.
Launch new ventures in weeks, not months
Acquire businesses and snap them into your chassis
Delegate execution to humans or AI
Build a portfolio, not a job
Turn identity into IP, not dependency
Turn this into a one-page master template
Create a Business Plug-In Spec (like an API doc)
Map this directly onto your current ecosystem
Tell me which artifact you want first.
Good — then we have to strip identity, industry, and founder psychology out of it entirely.
What you’re not building is a business framework.
You’re building a universal execution substrate that any business model can run on.
Below is the irreducible, model-agnostic version.
No creator bias. No SaaS bias. No service bias. No founder heroics.
A business is a state-transition machine.
If that cannot be precisely defined, the “business” does not exist — only activity does.
This is the only universal definition.
Every business, regardless of model, decomposes into five atoms:
If you remove any one, the system collapses.
Purpose: Detect and capture external signals.
This is not marketing. It is environment sensing.
Someone outside the system must want change
The system must detect that want
Purpose: Change state in a predictable way.
INPUT STATE → RULED PROCESS → OUTPUT STATE
If a human must “figure it out each time,” the value engine is invalid.
No execution step may depend on personality, memory, or heroics.
If it does, it is not a system — it is a bet on a human.
Feedback is what makes the system alive.
Purpose: Decide what is allowed to exist.
Without governance, systems rot or metastasize.
8. The Universal Stack (Applies to All Business Models)
Every business is simply this stack:
A business is truly universal and modular if:
You can replace the founder without redefining the system
You can replace the offer without changing the structure
You can replace the execution agents (human → AI → machine)
You can pause growth without system failure
You can explain the system without referencing a personality
Fail any one → it’s bespoke, not universal.
Because it is not a business model.
11. What You Can Build On Top of This
Once this substrate exists, you can layer:
If you want to operationalize this, I can produce:
A single-page Universal Business Spec (like an RFC)
A Notion / Obsidian schema that enforces universality
A venture ingestion checklist for acquisitions or launches
A machine-readable version for AI agents
Tell me which layer you want instantiated first.

Understood. I’ll remove metaphorical or founder-culture language and restate the system using formal, professional, enterprise-grade terminology consistent with strategy consulting, operations engineering, and corporate governance.
Below is the fully universal, model-agnostic, founder-independent Business Templatization Framework, written in expert business language.
A business is an organized value-creation system that:
Measures performance against objective criteria, and
Governs continuation, modification, or termination through formal decision rules.
This definition applies uniformly across all industries, legal structures, and operating models.
All businesses decompose into five irreducible functional domains:
These domains are invariant. Only their internal configurations differ.
To identify, capture, and qualify external demand signals in a measurable and repeatable manner.
External Environment → Demand Signal → Qualified Opportunity
Operates independently of delivery or execution
Converts unstructured market signals into standardized inputs
The demand interface is responsible for signal qualification, not persuasion or fulfillment.
To define the specific transformation the business provides.
Initial State → Defined Process → Target State
Explicit definition of input and output conditions
If the transformation cannot be clearly articulated or measured, the business lacks a valid value creation model.
To convert resources into outcomes in accordance with the value creation logic.
Execution must be process-dependent, not person-dependent.
To ensure operational accuracy, efficiency, and continuous improvement.
These metrics apply regardless of industry or business model and form the basis of operational control.
To provide formal oversight, risk management, and strategic control.
Policy Framework → Decision Rights → Resource Allocation
Governance ensures the business remains aligned with strategic and financial objectives.
This architecture is invariant across all business models.
A business system is considered fully templatized when:
Leadership can be replaced without redesigning operations
Offers can be modified without structural changes
Execution resources (human, automated, or AI-driven) are interchangeable
Growth can be paused without system failure
The business can be transferred or replicated without tacit knowledge
Failure to meet any criterion indicates residual dependence on individual discretion rather than system design.
The organization evolves from operating individual businesses to managing standardized value systems.
If desired, I can formalize this into:
A standardized business intake and evaluation framework
A Notion or Obsidian-based operating schema
A venture acquisition and integration checklist
A machine-readable specification for AI or automation systems
Please indicate which artifact you want produced first.
